name: tgo
description: Open-source AI Agent Customer Service Platform. Build AI agent teams with LLM orchestration, RAG knowledge base, multi-channel support, and human collaboration.

commit.message:
feat: add tgo-widget-flutter SDK, update READMEs with iOS and Flutter widgets

Add tgo-widget-flutter cross-platform customer service chat widget for iOS
and Android. Update all README translations (EN/CN/TC/JP/RU) to include
tgo-widget-ios and tgo-widget-flutter in the Widget SDKs table.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

chore(tgo-widget-ios): add .gitignore for Xcode and SPM build artifacts

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

feat: add tgo-widget-ios Swift SDK with example app

iOS 15+ SwiftUI-based customer service widget matching tgo-widget-js API contracts.
Includes models, services (API/IM/upload/streaming), state management, chat UI,
UIKit bridging, i18n (en/zh), unit tests, and an Example Xcode project demonstrating
modal, embedded SwiftUI, and pre-configured integration patterns.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

docs: move System Requirements to bottom, update to 4 Core / 8 GiB

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

docs: move Quick Start section right after TGO Introduction in all READMEs

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix(ci): skip npm publish when version already exists on registry

Compare local package.json version with npm registry before publishing
to avoid duplicate version errors. Simplify paths trigger to catch all
changes under the widget-miniprogram directory.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

ci: add GitHub Actions workflow for tgo-widget-miniprogram npm publishing

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

feat: rename tgo-widget-app to tgo-widget-js, add repo structure tables to READMEs

- Rename tgo-widget-app to tgo-widget-js to avoid confusion with native app
- Add Repository Structure tables to all 5 README files (EN/CN/TC/JP/RU)
- Split Widget SDKs into a separate sub-table
- Update all references across docker-compose, Makefile, scripts, docs, and source code

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

feat: update AI provider default models to latest versions

Refresh seed data with current model IDs: GPT-5/5.4, o4-mini, Claude 4.x,
Qwen 3.5, Kimi K2/K2.5, Llama 3.3, and human-readable display names.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

feat: add DB-backed default model templates for AI providers

When creating a provider without explicit available_models, the system
now seeds common models from the new api_ai_provider_default_models
table (migration 0026). This removes the need for callers to know
provider-specific model lists upfront and auto-selects a default_model.
The frontend toast now reports how many models were initialized.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

feat: add functional-verification/local-services skills, adopt $skill-name policies

Add two new skills: functional-verification (CLI-based runtime API smoke
tests) and local-services (start/stop/status for dev services). Add YAML
frontmatter to all SKILL.md files. Rewrite root AGENTS.md from flat rules
to Policies & Mandatory Skills format with per-skill trigger/skip conditions
and a 10-step Development Workflow. Add CLI verification commands to 8
service AGENTS.md files.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

feat: add tgo-cli source and Makefile build targets

Add tgo-cli project files and Makefile targets for install-cli
and build-cli.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

feat(widget-cli): add visitor-facing CLI + MCP Server

Implements tgo-widget-cli with Platform API Key auth, visitor
registration, SSE streaming chat, WuKongIM WebSocket, message
history, file upload, activity recording, and 10 MCP tools.
Collects real system info (Node version, OS, hostname, timezone)
during visitor registration.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

feat: add .skills/ system and rewrite AGENTS.md to rule-driven format

Introduce deterministic skill scripts (.skills/) for code verification,
implementation analysis, PR summary, migration checks, cross-service sync,
and streaming protocol checks. Rewrite root and all 14 service AGENTS.md
files from narrative docs to concise if/then rules + key paths + constraints
format, improving signal-to-noise ratio for AI agents.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

feat: upgrade deps, improve json-render and stream processing across projects

- Makefile: fix VITE_WIDGET_PREVIEW_URL typo, add --legacy-peer-deps
- tgo-ai: improve spec stream prompt (require root, self-contained specs)
- tgo-api: add platform model field, update chat endpoint
- tgo-web: upgrade json-render/core, improve JSONRenderSurface action
  handling, clean up stream_data references, add STREAM_PROCESSING docs
- tgo-widget-app: align json-render with tgo-web, remove legacy stream_data
- All Python services: update poetry.lock dependencies
- All JS projects: update lock files

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

feat(widget-miniprogram): add miniprogram chat widget with CI and AGENTS.md

Initial commit of the WeChat miniprogram native chat widget ported from
tgo-widget-app. Includes json-render system, miniprogram-ci verification,
build pipeline with zod .cjs fix, and comprehensive AGENTS.md documentation.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

docs(widget-app): update AGENTS.md with json-render system documentation

Add comprehensive json-render architecture docs, component registry
reference, streaming mechanisms, and tgo-web sync rules.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

feat(widget-app): align json-render with tgo-web streaming fixes

Port all json-render bug fixes from tgo-web to tgo-widget-app:
- Add HandlerSync to register action handlers during streaming
- Add statePath detection to distinguish state updates from submits
- Filter builtin actions, add null element guard in collectActionNames
- Use createStateStore for proper state management
- Add loading prop to suppress missing-element warnings during streaming
- Support prop aliases in Checkbox, DateTimeInput, MultipleChoice
- Support string option shorthand in MultipleChoice
- Add mode/type prop support in DateTimeInput
- Add loading state to Button component

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

feat: unify mixed stream parsing and remove legacy stream_data field

Replace split stream architecture (separate text + json_render events) with
unified MixedStreamParser that preserves interleaved text/spec order. Remove
stream_data from API responses in favor of event_meta snapshots.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
feat: upgrade wukongim sdk

feat: migrate chat rendering to json-render and harden chat fallbacks

Migrate tgo-web and tgo-widget-app from legacy A2UI widgets to json-render, including streaming patch rendering surfaces, message integration, and parser cleanup.

Remove deprecated tgo-ai ui_templates stack, add json-render schema/validator/parser assets, and align runtime streaming/team models with the new render protocol.

Fix tgo-api /v1/chat/completion behavior for wukongim_only flows so responses do not block on stream startup, and make MCP toolkit initialization failures degrade gracefully instead of aborting output.

Disable verbose SQL echo logging in tgo-api database engines and refresh related configs, stores, websocket handling, lockfiles, and service AGENTS docs.
update github action
update github action
update poetry.lock

feat: add tgo-device-agent and update RAG service configuration

- Implement `tgo-device-agent` in Go, providing file operations (read/write/edit) and shell execution tools for AI agents via TCP JSON-RPC 2.0.
- Add security sandbox for `tgo-device-agent` with path whitelisting and command blacklisting.
- Update `tgo-rag` service to support configurable default QA mode and change its default port to 18082 to avoid conflicts.
- Remove obsolete `docs/GET_REAL_IP.md` and update environment templates.

feat: implement AI skills system and refactor device control

- Implement AI Skills system using Agno (formerly Phidata) for dynamic tool loading and system prompt injection.
- Add `SkillFileService` and `GithubSkillDownloader` for managing and downloading skill packages.
- Refactor Device Control by removing redundant model configurations and integrating device binding directly into AI agents.
- Add database migrations for skill enablement and device control schema updates.
- Update frontend with new Skill management UI components (SkillCard, SkillFormModal, etc.) and localized strings.
- Enhance `AgentBuilder` to support skill loading and automatic tool registration for agents.

Refactor device control and integrate device binding into AI agents

- **tgo-device-control**: Remove legacy AgentOS architecture and simplify MCP server implementation. Delete unused schemas, services, and API endpoints related to the old computer use agent.
- **tgo-ai**: Add `bound_device_id` to Agent model and schemas. Include database migration for the new field. Update agent builder to support device-bound agents.
- **tgo-web**: Remove deprecated Device Debug interface and related stores. Update Agent creation/editing flows to support device selection. Add new UI components for displaying bound devices.
- **tgo-api**: Update AI agent endpoints to handle device binding fields.

Co-authored-by: Cursor <cursoragent@cursor.com>

feat(device-control): add model config and device debug chat

- Add project-level device control model config (provider_id, model) in
  project_ai_config and API; add device-level AI model config
  (ai_provider_id, model) on dc_devices
- Add device debug chat: streaming /device-control/chat endpoint,
  DeviceDebugChat UI with messages, screenshots, execution timeline,
  and device info sidebar; route /ai/device-debug/:deviceId and debug
  entry from DeviceCard
- Extend device control API and client for debug chat and config;
  update tgo-ai chat schema and service for device control model
- Add Alembic migrations: 0024 (api_project_ai_configs),
  0003 (dc_devices)
- i18n and UI updates for device control and device debug (en/zh)

fix: reduce deadlocks on api_visitors updates

- Update visitor message stats using FOR UPDATE SKIP LOCKED to avoid waiting on rows locked by transfer flows.
- Commit earlier in transfer_to_staff to release the visitor FOR UPDATE lock before channel-member side effects.
update github action

feat: implement computer-use agent and TCP-based device control

- Introduce `AgentCategory` (normal, computer_use) to differentiate agent types.
- Add validation for computer-use agents: must bind exactly one device and cannot bind tools/knowledge/workflows.
- Refactor `tgo-device-control` to use TCP RPC and connection management instead of WebSockets.
- Implement remote agent endpoints and device selection UI in the frontend.
- Add database migrations for agent category and device management updates.
feat: enhance knowledge search with multi-content type filtering
feat: add device-control microservice and computer-use integration

feat: enhance RAG processing visibility and AI sync error handling

- tgo-rag: Add migration to enable pg_trgm extension for improved search.
- tgo-ai: Improve error logging and exception details during LLM model and provider sync.
- tgo-web: Add support for more granular RAG processing statuses (chunking, embedding) in UI.
- i18n: Update English and Chinese locales with new status labels and store messages.

refactor(tgo-web): enhance AI store and model management

- Improve StoreLoginModal flow to prevent popup blockers by opening a blank window first.
- Use useCallback for category fetching in Store modals to optimize performance.
- Add stricter typing for model types ('chat' | 'embedding') in AddModelModal and providersStore.
- Refine model merging logic in providersStore when adding new models to a provider.

feat: enhance AI provider model management

- Backend: Update AI provider update logic to handle model type information in available_models.
- Backend: Introduce AIModelInput schema for structured model input.
- Frontend: Add model deletion capability to ProviderCard component.
- Frontend: Update providersStore and aiProvidersApi to support model removal.
- Frontend: Add English and Chinese translations for model management actions and toasts.

refactor(tgo-web): modularize AI Store components

- Extract common UI patterns into base components under src/components/ai/store/base/
- Implement StoreCardBase, StoreContentArea, StoreDetailPanel, StoreHeader, StoreModalBase, StoreSidebar, and StoreUserStatus
- Introduce useStoreModal hook for shared store logic
- Refactor AgentStore, ModelStore, and ToolStore components to use the new modular base components
- Improve consistency and maintainability across all store-related UI

refactor: simplify configuration by consolidating env files into root .env

- Remove service-specific env files in envs.docker/ and envs.example/
- Update docker-compose.yml to use root .env for all services
- Update .env.example with SMTP and advanced settings
- Simplify tgo.sh to only manage the root .env file
- Enhance frontend store components with improved modal and detail views

feat: update store components to use API proxy

- Update storeApi.ts to use proxy routing through tgo-api instead of direct backend calls
- Enhance store modal components with logout and credit card functionality
- Update i18n locales and constants for proxy integration
- Modify docker entrypoint and API services for proxy support

feat: implement store API proxy functionality

- Add proxy endpoint in store.py to route requests through tgo-api
- Update storeApi.ts to use proxy routing instead of direct backend calls
- Enhance store-related components with proxy integration
- Update configuration and services for proxy support

refactor(tgo-ai): modularize agent and team builders for better maintainability

- Refactor  and  to use smaller, specialized helper methods for building local/remote agents and tools.
- Improve error handling and logging across agent/team construction processes.
- Clean up imports and remove redundant inline imports in .
- Enhance UI template tool integration with clearer function names and docstrings.
- Standardize configuration normalization and model initialization logic.

- repos/tgo-api: expand store API endpoints for dependency resolution and implement AI model synchronization
- repos/tgo-web: add AgentDependencyModal and optimize Store modals to handle complex agent installations with dependencies

feat: implement Agent Store integration

- repos/tgo-ai: add remote store agent fields to models and schemas, update agent builder, and add database migration
- repos/tgo-api: implement agent store endpoints, schemas, and store client integration
- repos/tgo-web: add Agent Store UI components (Card, Detail, Modal) and integrate with store API

             ^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^

feat: implement Tool Store integration with OAuth/PKCE authentication

- repos/tgo-api: add toolstore endpoints, models, and credential management

feat: implement AI Tool Store feature

- Added Tool Store modal for discovering and installing AI tool extensions.
- Integrated Tool Store button into the AI Tools management page.
- Added detailed tool view and installation flow.
- Implemented login requirement for tool installation.
- Updated internationalization for both English and Chinese.
- Added Tool Store API documentation.

feat: refactor for SaaS extension support and modernize application initialization

- repos/tgo-api: refactor app creation into factory function create_app to allow router/middleware injection
- repos/tgo-plugin-runtime: migrate to FastAPI lifespan context manager
- repos/tgo-web: implement dynamic SaaS route injection and add Vite aliases for SaaS builds

refactor: centralize MessageType as IntEnum and add AI agent guide

- Relocate MessageType to app/utils/const.py and convert to IntEnum for improved type safety.
- Update ChatCompletionRequest schema and chat_service to utilize the new enum.
- Add AGENTS.md as a comprehensive guide for AI coding assistants.
- Standardize message type handling by replacing magic numbers with enum constants.
- Cleanup: remove obsolete DEVELOPMENT.md.

  - Support respond_directly, num_history_runs, markdown, etc., in Team and Agent builders.

  - Update API services, stores, and hooks to handle config data flow.

readme_html:
TGO Introduction

TGO is an open-source AI agent customer service platform dedicated to helping enterprises "Build AI Agent Teams for Customer Service". It integrates multi-channel access, agent orchestration, knowledge base management (RAG), and human agent collaboration.

🚀 Quick Start

One-Click Deployment

Run the following command on your server to check requirements, clone the repository, and start the services:

REF=latest curl -fsSL https://raw.githubusercontent.com/tgoai/tgo/main/bootstrap.sh | bash

For users in China (using Gitee and Aliyun mirrors):

REF=latest curl -fsSL https://gitee.com/tgoai/tgo/raw/main/bootstrap_cn.sh | bash

For more details, please visit the Documentation.

✨ Features

🤖 AI Agent Orchestration

  • Multi-Agent Support - Configure multiple AI agents for different business scenarios
  • Multi-Model Integration - Connect with various LLM providers (OpenAI, Anthropic, etc.)
  • Streaming Response - Real-time AI responses via SSE for smooth conversation experience
  • Context Memory - Maintain conversation history for coherent dialogue

📚 Knowledge Base (RAG)

  • Document Knowledge Base - Upload documents to enhance AI response accuracy
  • Q&A Knowledge Base - Create question-answer pairs for quick knowledge expansion
  • Website Knowledge Base - Crawl website content to keep information up-to-date
  • Smart Retrieval - Vector-based semantic search for precise answers

🔧 MCP Tools Integration

  • Tool Store - Rich library of MCP tools, enable on demand
  • Custom Tools - Project-level tool configuration and management
  • OpenAPI Schema - Auto-parse schemas to generate interactive forms

🌐 Multi-Channel Access

  • Web Widget - Embeddable chat widget for websites
  • WeChat Integration - Official Account and Mini Program support
  • Unified Management - Manage all channels from a single dashboard

💬 Real-time Communication

  • WuKongIM Integration - Stable and reliable instant messaging
  • WebSocket Connection - Efficient bidirectional communication
  • Message Sync - Read/unread status, delivery confirmation
  • Rich Media - Support for text, images, files and more

👥 Human-AI Collaboration

  • Smart Handoff - Seamlessly transfer to human agents when needed
  • Visitor Management - Collect visitor info, assign sessions, track history
  • Agent Workspace - Unified interface for human agents

🎨 UI Widget System

  • Structured Display - Render orders, products, logistics as beautiful cards
  • Rich Components - Order cards, logistics tracking, product display, price comparison
  • Action Protocol - Standardized URI protocol for interactions

📦 Repository Structure

Repository Description Tech Stack
tgo-ai AI/ML operations service for managing agents, tool bindings, knowledge bases, and usage analytics Python / FastAPI
tgo-api Core business logic service handling user management, visitor tracking, assignment, and communication Python / FastAPI
tgo-cli CLI tool & MCP Server enabling AI agents to execute customer service operations with 40+ built-in tools TypeScript / Node.js
tgo-device-agent Embedded agent running on managed devices providing file and shell capabilities via TCP JSON-RPC Go
tgo-device-control Device control service managing TCP/JSON-RPC connections for remote device management with MCP Agent Python / FastAPI
tgo-platform Multi-channel message intake service supporting WeChat, Feishu, DingTalk, Telegram, Slack, email, etc. Python / FastAPI
tgo-plugin-runtime Plugin lifecycle management and execution service with dynamic tool synchronization Python / FastAPI
tgo-rag RAG service providing document processing, hybrid semantic/full-text search, and async processing Python / FastAPI
tgo-web Admin frontend with real-time chat, AI agent management, knowledge base, and MCP tool integration TypeScript / React 19
tgo-workflow AI Agent workflow execution engine supporting DAG topology with LLM, API, condition, and tool nodes Python / FastAPI
